compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The path-hardening change under a9d0 (merged 0209c5a2) genuinely remediates the
path-injection vulnerabilities (tests prove traversal is blocked) and cleared the
`py/stack-trace-exposure` alert — but the 27 `py/path-injection` code-scanning alerts did
NOT clear, because CodeQL's dataflow model did not recognize the containment barriers.

Verified locally against the CodeQL CLI (2.26.1, python-queries 1.8.6) by reading the model
source (`semmle/python/security/dataflow/PathInjectionQuery.qll` + `Concepts.qll` +
`frameworks/Stdlib.qll`): the ONLY recognized path-injection sanitizer is
`os.path.normpath|abspath|realpath` (`Path::PathNormalization`) followed by a simple
`.startswith(prefix)` guard (`Path::SafeAccessCheck`, which validates the object only on the
`startswith == True` branch). Two things defeated recognition:

1. `_existing_ticket_dir_name` guarded with `if candidate != tracker_norm and not
   candidate.startswith(...)`. The `!= tracker_norm` disjunct means the safe (fall-through)
   branch is `candidate == tracker_norm OR startswith(...)`, so CodeQL cannot prove
   `startswith` held there → the barrier does not apply.
2. `editor_server._raw_prompt_text` used `Path.resolve()` + `Path.is_relative_to()`, neither of
   which CodeQL models as PathNormalization / SafeAccessCheck.

## What

Reshape both guards into the exact recognized pattern (normpath + simple startswith); behavior
is unchanged (still rejects `../x`, absolute, separators, dotfiles):

- `_ids.py` `_existing_ticket_dir_name`: drop the `!= tracker_norm` disjunct →
  `if not candidate.startswith(tracker_norm + os.sep): return None`. This one change cascades:
  the reducer read path, `reads.py`, and the three sidecars all consume the resolver's return,
  so recognizing the resolver's barrier clears all of their sinks too.
- `editor_server.py` `_raw_prompt_text`: replace `resolve()`/`is_relative_to` with
  `os.path.normpath` + `if user.startswith(prompts_dir + os.sep) and os.path.isfile(user)` (add
  `import os`).

Validated with the CodeQL CLI on a clean `git archive` tree: all 28 previously-open server
sinks (`reads.py`, `_ids.py`, `reducer/*`, both sidecars, `editor_server`) report 0 after the
change. (14 local-only-source results remain — `fsutil`, prompt library/authoring — that the
server's threat model never reported and are out of scope here.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_existing_ticket_dir_name` uses a simple `os.path.normpath` + `startswith(tracker_dir +
      os.sep)` containment guard with no disjunct that defeats CodeQL's SafeAccessCheck barrier.
- [ ] `editor_server._raw_prompt_text` contains the read with `os.path.normpath` + `startswith`
      (not `Path.is_relative_to`), and imports `os`.
- [ ] Behavior is unchanged: valid full/short/alias/Jira/prefix ids and prompt ids still
      resolve/read; traversal/absolute inputs are still refused. The existing
      `tests/unit/test_path_injection_guards.py` suite passes; `make lint` + mypy pass.
- [ ] Local CodeQL `py/path-injection` analysis reports 0 for all 28 previously-open server
      sinks; after landing, the GitHub code-scanning `py/path-injection` count reaches 0.
compiled_state.title: Reshape path-injection guards into CodeQL-recognized normpath+startswith form (clear the 27 py/path-injection alerts)
